lesson.title: Lesson 12: Life on a Desert Island

rights.notes: Migrated from legacy published content; migration does not establish redistribution rights.

segments.text: Lesson 12.

translation.text: 第12课。

segments.text: Life on a Desert Island.

translation.text: 荒岛生活。

segments.text: Listen to the tape, then answer the question below.

translation.text: 听录音，然后回答下面的问题。

segments.text: What was exceptional about the two men's stay on the desert island?

translation.text: 这两个人在荒岛上的经历有什么不同寻常之处？

segments.text: Most of us have formed an unrealistic picture of life on a desert island.

translation.text: 我们大多数人对荒岛生活都有一种不切实际的想象。

segments.text: We sometimes imagine a desert island to be a sort of paradise where the sun always shines.

translation.text: 我们有时把荒岛想成阳光永远灿烂的天堂。

segments.text: Life there is simple and good.

translation.text: 那里的生活简单而美好。

segments.text: Ripe fruit falls from the trees, and you never have to work.

translation.text: 成熟的水果从树上掉下来，人们永远不必劳动。

segments.text: The other side of the picture is quite the opposite.

translation.text: 另一幅图景却完全相反。

segments.text: Life on a desert island is wretched.

translation.text: 荒岛生活悲惨不堪。

segments.text: You either starve to death or live like Robinson Crusoe, waiting for a boat which never comes.

translation.text: 你不是饿死，就是像鲁滨孙·克鲁索一样生活，等待一艘永远不会来的船。

segments.text: Perhaps there is an element of truth in both these pictures, but few of us have had the opportunity to find out.

translation.text: 也许这两幅图景都含有几分真实，但我们很少有人有机会亲自验证。

segments.text: Two men, who recently spent five days on a coral island, wished they had stayed there longer.

translation.text: 最近有两个人在一座珊瑚岛上度过了五天，他们倒希望在那里待得更久些。

segments.text: They were taking a badly damaged boat from the Virgin Islands to Miami to have it repaired.

translation.text: 他们正把一艘严重受损的船从维尔京群岛驶往迈阿密修理。

segments.text: During the journey, their boat began to sink.

translation.text: 途中，他们的船开始下沉。

segments.text: They quickly loaded a small rubber dinghy with food, matches, and cans of beer, and rode for a few miles across the Caribbean until they arrived at a tiny coral island.

translation.text: 他们迅速把食物、火柴和罐装啤酒装进一艘小橡皮艇，在加勒比海上划了几英里，最终抵达一座小珊瑚岛。

segments.text: There were hardly any trees on the island, and there was no water, but this did not prove to be a problem.

translation.text: 岛上几乎没有树，也没有淡水，但这并没有成为问题。

segments.text: The men collected rainwater in the rubber dinghy.

translation.text: 他们用橡皮艇收集雨水。

segments.text: As they had brought a spear gun with them, they had plenty to eat.

translation.text: 由于带着一支捕鱼枪，他们有充足的食物。

segments.text: They caught lobster and fish every day, and, as one of them put it, ate like kings.

translation.text: 他们每天捕捉龙虾和鱼，用其中一人的话说，吃得像国王一样。

segments.text: When a passing tanker rescued them five days later, both men were genuinely sorry that they had to leave.

translation.text: 五天后，一艘路过的油轮救起他们时，两人都真心为不得不离开而感到遗憾。
